author: @darrellpidgeon6440
text: My Dad would have loved seeing this.  Working with wood was such a big hobby for him.  He actually got to the point where he made some money doing cabinet work.  BTW, the title here made me chuckle for some reason.  "Piston and Crank" sounds almost like a couple of cartoon characters...lol.

author: @scotttovey
text:
Let's see; 

-if you make a solid piston cylinder
-attach your valve mechanism
-add a bit of compressed air 

You would have an air powered crank.

Cool demo Rob.

author: @sofa-lofa4241
text:
Nice tip for cheap materials... I've used pound shop nylon chopping boards for a good cheap source of almost indestructible sheet plastic, easily cut, bent, drilled and tapped,
Nice grain on those, would look great with a bit of Danish oil or similar finish.

author: @ThisTall
text:
Would love to see you experiment with an HHO generator ppl use in their cars. I spent 3 years trying to make one and use in my car. What I learned was that 99% of what is claimed is garbage BUT that it can work and be totally worth while. I made my 2006 Mazda3 go from 25mpg to 32mpg. But what that system really is, is just a fuel catalyst generator you can get moderate gains with by using no more then 3-5amps to produce hydrogen that then just helps your fuel combust much better. The HHO community is rightfully mocked in my opinion because they all seem to claim or strongly imply you create large amounts and run off it or even majority run off it. 

If you’re interested I could share a bit more about my setup for a possible experiment?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
